{{Short description|Italian film festival in 2004}} {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2021}} {{Infobox film or theatre festival | name = 61st Venice International Film Festival | image = VeniceFilmFestival61poster.jpg | caption = Festival poster | opening = ''[[The Terminal]]'' | closing = ''[[Steamboy]]'' | location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] | founded = 1932 | awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[Vera Drake]]'' |artistic_director= Marco Müller | host = [[Claudia Gerini]] | number = | date = 1–11 September 2004 | website = [http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/ Website] | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[60th Venice International Film Festival|60th]] | followed_by = [[62nd Venice International Film Festival|62nd]] }} The '''61st annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''', was held from 1 and 11 September 2004, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]].{{cite web |title=Venice – 2004 Ecco il Leone |url=http://www.americancinemapapers.com/files/VENICE_2004.htm |access-date=6 October 2013}} British filmmaker [[John Boorman]] was the jury president for the main competition. The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[Vera Drake]]'', directed by [[Mike Leigh]].{{cite web |date=19 April 2010 |title=The 2000s |url=http://www.carnivalofvenice.com/?page_id=503&lang=en |access-date=8 October 2013}} On this edition, a new independent section, Venice Days (''[[Giornate degli Autori|Giornate degli autori]]''), was created "for free cinema, new talents and new stories". This section is organized by the ANAC (National Association of Cinematographic Authors) and the API (Independent Authors & Producers).{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/line-up/index.html |title=Line-up of the 61st Venice Film Festival |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040822021356/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/line-up/index.html |archive-date=22 August 2004 |access-date=5 June 2018 }} Also in this edition, ''The Secret History of Italian Cinema'' was launched, a new official retrospective section, with the aim of restoring and systematically rediscovering sides of the Italian cinema that have been "forgotten, invisible, unknown or misunderstood". The retrospective was planned for the following four editions (61st to 64th editions), later was extended with the ''These Phantoms: Italian Cinema Rediscovered'' (65th edition), and the ''Italian Comedy Retrospective'' (66th edition). The [[Fondazione Prada]] was the main sponsor/partner of this section.{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/prada.html |title=Fondazione Prada is the partner of "The Secret History of Italian Cinema" at the 61st Venice Film Festival |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040906075743/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/prada.html |archive-date=6 September 2004 |access-date=6 June 2018 }} The festival opened with ''[[The Terminal]]'' by [[Steven Spielberg]],{{cite web |title=61st Venice Film Festival - Wednesday, September 1, 2004 |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/screenings/sept1.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040822025445/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/screenings/sept1.html |archive-date=22 August 2004 |access-date=5 June 2018 |work=labiennale.org}} and closed with ''[[Steamboy]]'' by [[Katsuhiro Otomo]].{{cite web |title=61st Venice Film Festival - Wednesday, September 11, 2004 |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/screenings/sept11.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040822100010/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/screenings/sept11.html |archive-date=22 August 2004 |access-date=5 June 2018 |work=labiennale.org}} ==Juries== === Main Competition (''Venezia 61'') === * [[John Boorman]], British filmmaker - Jury President{{cite web |title=The international juries of the 61st Venice Film Festival |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/juries.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040914023549/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/61miac/juries.html |archive-date=14 September 2004 |access-date=4 June 2018 |work=labiennale.org}}{{cite web |title=Juries for the 2000s |url=http://www.carnivalofvenice.com/?page_id=527&lang=en |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170804133752/http://www.carnivalofvenice.com/?page_id=527&lang=en |archive-date=4 August 2017 |access-date=4 April 2018}} * [[Wolfgang Becker (director, born 1954)|Wolfgang Becker]], German filmmaker * [[Mimmo Calopresti]], Italian director * [[Hsu Feng]], Taiwanese actress and producer * [[Scarlett Johansson]], American actress * [[Spike Lee]], American filmmaker, actor and producer * [[Dušan Makavejev|Dusan Makavejev]], Serbian filmmaker * [[Helen Mirren]], British actress * [[Pietro Scalia]], Italian editor === ''[[Orizzonti]]'' === * [[Nicolas Philibert]], French director -
